scored_hypotheses.claim: The text uses a simple substitution cipher on medieval Latin, which would produce glyph entropy ~4.0

test_result.test: claude_evaluation
test_result.details: Glyph entropy (3.86 bits) matches predicted ~4.0 bits, but the extremely high hapax ratio (0.701) and substandard Zipf exponent (0.8946) contradict the expected statistical profile of a medieval Latin substitution cipher, suggesting the underlying text is either non-linguistic, non-Latin, or more complex than a simple cipher.
